title: Rich Gooey Japanese Rice Dumplings
description: A weeknight Japanese dish from the Kanto region featuring vanilla sugar and a moist, velvety texture. Perfect for a comforting dinner or snack.

ingredients.name: Vanilla sugar
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality vanilla sugar for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Mushroom dashi
ingredients.notes: You can use store-bought dashi or make it from scratch.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the gluten-free flour, rice flour, granulated sugar, and salt.
Add the egg and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it's smooth and elastic.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Place the balls of dough on a lightly floured surface and flatten them slightly into discs.
Place a small spoonful of the mushroom dashi in the center of each disc and fold the dough over to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Place the dumplings on a bamboo steamer lined with parchment paper and steam for 8-10 minutes.
While the dumplings are steaming, mix the milk and vanilla sugar in a small bowl.
Once the dumplings are cooked, brush them with the milk-vanilla sugar mixture and serve hot.
Serve the rich gooey rice dumplings with additional mushroom dashi for dipping, if desired.
